_readme: Map patches: small, typed fixes applied as the FINAL world-gen step. Each entry: {id, why, ops:[...]}. Ops: {op:'terrain', rect:[x0,y0,x1,y1]|cells:[[x,y]..], to:'GRASS'} | {op:'trail', from:[x,y], to:[x,y]} | {op:'remove_objects', rect:[x0,y0,x1,y1], types:['decor','resource','structure']} | {op:'object', x,y, label, color, examine} | {op:'decor', x,y, color, size, shape}. Terrain names must exist in worldData T. See docs/MAP_DESIGN_PASS.md.
_notes: MACRO-BLOCKED CHUNKS (do NOT force these defect counts down with the local patch loop — they need the macro connectivity/bridge pass, CRITICAL_PATH.md Steps 4-5): The whole NW 'Goblin Wilds' north of the Willow river is a large region stranded from spawn (audit_world warns ~7642-tile pocket near 72,106). Because of it: c0,r1 has dead_pocket (67,105) size 424 — reachable across a single river channel at (58,124)→(58,125), so ONE authored footbridge/ford reclaims all 424 tiles; and sealed_room (80,129) sits INSIDE the stranded region, so punching a door won't un-seal it until the region connects. The treeline slivers at x70-74 / x78-79 y135-199 are a dense 2-3-deep wall on the river's west bank; their sealed trees only un-seal by punching multiple gaps, low value until the bridge lands. RECOMMEND: the macro pass decides where the Wilds bridge goes (a ford near x58,y124 is the natural spot), then the local loop mops up the slivers. Until then leave c0,r1 as-is.

patches.why: Unreachable 1-9 tile walkable slivers in the west mountain shoulder (defect scanner: dead_pocket around 79-86,203-239). Filled to rock — they were never meant to be stood on.

MACRO connectivity (owner-approved): the 7642-tile north 'Goblin Wilds' was stranded from spawn (audit_world warning). Its whole region touches reachable ground across ONE shallow-water tile at (74,149) — the natural narrowest crossing, flanked by deep water. A footbridge/ford here reclaims all ~7643 tiles, giving the Wilds its entrance and un-stranding the sealed_room + treeline slivers for local cleanup. trail lays dirt approaches + a bridge (runs before the texture pass, so the ford tile is still base WATER and bridges cleanly).

MACRO connectivity (owner-approved): six more wilderness regions were fully stranded from spawn, each cut off by a single narrow river/swamp channel. Each ford is the region's NARROWEST crossing (water-only, no rock carved) so it reads as a natural footbridge/ford, not a gash. Reclaims ~7045 tiles (2815+1282+1187+786+551+424). The 424 ford at (70,107) also clears c0,r1's dead_pocket (67,105). Skipped: a 225-tile SE-corner islet at (995,99x) that needs a 4-tile span to the map edge — left for review (may be an intentional offshore feature).

c1,r3: three hollow diamond rings of stray WALL tiles (no label, no floor, no structure = generation artifacts, not authored ruins), each flagged as ~8 wall_orphans enclosing a 5-tile dead_pocket. Convert each ring footprint to GRASS — clears the orphan walls and opens the enclosed pocket at once. Rects contain only wall/grass/decor (no water/rock/POI), so the fill is safe.

c0,r2 (Goblin Wilds mountain edge): 8 dead_pockets + 43 sealed trees. The pockets are tree-walled grass courtyards pinned between the crag and the Willow river; a Charred Homestead and a Dropped Coin Purse were sealed inside/on the rock. Fix = open the treelines so each courtyard (and its POI) becomes reachable, clear the ~20 trees stranded on the bare rock shoulder, and cut a dirt doorstep to the coin purse the earlier rock-fill had buried. Removal-only + one un-rock cell, so no new slivers/orphans possible.
